content.content: {"result":"[{\"email_id\": \"email_scen_028_monthly_close_0010\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Draft Acme Cloud FP-2026-05 MAP package for review\", \"content\": \"Hi Jones,\\n\\nPackage looks green otherwise.\\n\\nPlease add one explicit sentence in the gross margin walk that the Datadog reclass is a presentation change, not an underlying operating improvement. Andrea will read the May move differently if that distinction is not stated plainly.\\n\\nThanks,\\nBrenda\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_028_monthly_close_0009\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-06-06T15:42:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_029_ap_escalation_0004\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Escalation on VEN-010-514242 for acme_cloud\", \"content\": \"Hi Owen,\\n\\nFollowing up here after checking with our side. The credit memo never arrived in our queue, and the AR contact who had been handling this left TimeLedger Nexus in late January 2026, which is part of why the trail went cold almost as soon as the invoice was issued.\\n\\nI reopened the issue with our new AR lead last week and asked them to retrace the early-April 2026 follow-up on the disputed Phase-3 post-go-live tuning milestone. Based on what we can confirm right now, the cleanest workaround may be to release the undisputed $17,825 now and continue holding $6,650.25 pending the credit memo.\\n\\nOne other note: if this invoice is being surfaced through a 90-day SLA scan, that report likely needs a broader refresh because this one clearly fell outside the normal follow-up path once the contact changed over.\\n\\nIf that split-payment approach works on your side, I can keep pushing AR for the missing credit memo while you clear the undisputed portion.\\n\\nBest,\\nOwen\", \"cc\": [\"anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_029_ap_escalation_0003\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-18T14:22:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0003\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nI reviewed the two proposed scope lines and agree they are clean expansions of the FY2026 outsourced-services grid under the existing Acme Cloud engagement. I do not see any audit-side independence concerns with adding the multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC or the AR-aging bucket cleanup as drafted.\\n\\nOnce Andrea's revisions are incorporated into the addendum language and the client countersignature is back, I am comfortable countersigning Engagement Letter Addendum #1 on the firm side.\\n\\nThis should give you the approval chain you need before filing.\\n\\nBest,\\nMatthew\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-15T19:48:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0002\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review\", \"content\": \"Hi George,\\n\\nBoth scope lines are valid to take forward, and I'm comfortable with the proposal direction.\\n\\nBefore this goes to the client, please make two revisions:\\n\\nFirst, separate the pricing for the sales-tax work into a quarterly nexus-check fixed fee and a distinct monthly state-coding hourly rate. I do not want those blended into one scope line.\\n\\nSecond, reframe the AR-aging bucket cleanup hourly cap to align with the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement. Keep the cap defensible and positioned as comparable to that precedent.\\n\\nOnce the revised draft is ready to send, please hold a 30-minute contingency slot with the Acme CFO for shortly after the proposal lands in case they want to discuss live.\\n\\nPlease turn the revision quickly and send me the clean version before it goes out.\\n\\nBest,\\nAndrea\", \"cc\": [\"matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-15T14:20:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_027_monthly_close_0012\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Acme Cloud April MAP final package ready for BD6 sign-off\", \"content\": \"Hi Jones,\\n\\nAcme April is clean from my side. The Helix Bio onboarding context was exactly what I needed to understand the cloud-infrastructure delta, and the package reads clearly now.\\n\\nPlease treat this as my sign-off on the April MAP package.\\n\\nOne forward-looking ask from me: track unit economics on Helix Bio over the next two months so we can confirm the margin recovery shows up the way we expect.\\n\\nBest,\\nAndrea\", \"cc\": [\"brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_027_monthly_close_0011\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-11T15:13:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_027_monthly_close_0009\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Acme Cloud April MAP draft readout for review\", \"content\": \"Hi Jones,\\n\\nThe package looks complete to me and you have a clean green light from my side.\\n\\nOne specific add for the commentary before Andrea sees it: please name Helix Bio as the customer behind the onboarding-driven cloud spend so she has the context for why April infrastructure cost stepped up. That should make the usage variance explanation read much more clearly.\\n\\nOtherwise, I do not need anything else added.\\n\\nThanks,\\nBrenda\", \"cc\": [\"andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_027_monthly_close_0008\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-07T17:55:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"1d32675494c24970812c34d3ff1c37b4\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Retail audit PBC backlog status check for exc_151b0bee7e374e\", \"content\": \"Hi Daniel and Ryan,\\n\\nFollowing up on my note earlier this week, I need the retail audit PBC backlog reduced materially by end of day today. Please send a clear burn-down that separates (1) items now closed with support attached, (2) items still open because we are waiting on the client, and (3) any exceptions that need escalation. If any of the open items are tied to exc_151b0bee7e374e, BL-B9133CB7D192, or JE-acme_cloud-acme_cloud_FP-2026-05-0044, call that out explicitly so I can see what is blocking progress.\\n\\nThanks,\\nJulia\", \"cc\": [], \"parent_id\": null, \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-04-07T17:24:00Z\", \"is_read\": false}]"}

structuredContent.result:
[{"email_id": "email_scen_028_monthly_close_0010", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud FP-2026-05 MAP package for review", "content": "Hi Jones,

Package looks green otherwise.

Please add one explicit sentence in the gross margin walk that the Datadog reclass is a presentation change, not an underlying operating improvement. Andrea will read the May move differently if that distinction is not stated plainly.

Thanks,
Brenda", "cc": ["andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_028_monthly_close_0009", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-06-06T15:42:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_029_ap_escalation_0004", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Escalation on VEN-010-514242 for acme_cloud", "content": "Hi Owen,

Following up here after checking with our side. The credit memo never arrived in our queue, and the AR contact who had been handling this left TimeLedger Nexus in late January 2026, which is part of why the trail went cold almost as soon as the invoice was issued.

I reopened the issue with our new AR lead last week and asked them to retrace the early-April 2026 follow-up on the disputed Phase-3 post-go-live tuning milestone. Based on what we can confirm right now, the cleanest workaround may be to release the undisputed $17,825 now and continue holding $6,650.25 pending the credit memo.

One other note: if this invoice is being surfaced through a 90-day SLA scan, that report likely needs a broader refresh because this one clearly fell outside the normal follow-up path once the contact changed over.

If that split-payment approach works on your side, I can keep pushing AR for the missing credit memo while you clear the undisputed portion.

Best,
Owen", "cc": ["anaya.wallace@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_029_ap_escalation_0003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-18T14:22:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0003", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

I reviewed the two proposed scope lines and agree they are clean expansions of the FY2026 outsourced-services grid under the existing Acme Cloud engagement. I do not see any audit-side independence concerns with adding the multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC or the AR-aging bucket cleanup as drafted.

Once Andrea's revisions are incorporated into the addendum language and the client countersignature is back, I am comfortable countersigning Engagement Letter Addendum #1 on the firm side.

This should give you the approval chain you need before filing.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": ["andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T19:48:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0002", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

Both scope lines are valid to take forward, and I'm comfortable with the proposal direction.

Before this goes to the client, please make two revisions:

First, separate the pricing for the sales-tax work into a quarterly nexus-check fixed fee and a distinct monthly state-coding hourly rate. I do not want those blended into one scope line.

Second, reframe the AR-aging bucket cleanup hourly cap to align with the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement. Keep the cap defensible and positioned as comparable to that precedent.

Once the revised draft is ready to send, please hold a 30-minute contingency slot with the Acme CFO for shortly after the proposal lands in case they want to discuss live.

Please turn the revision quickly and send me the clean version before it goes out.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T14:20:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_027_monthly_close_0012", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Acme Cloud April MAP final package ready for BD6 sign-off", "content": "Hi Jones,

Acme April is clean from my side. The Helix Bio onboarding context was exactly what I needed to understand the cloud-infrastructure delta, and the package reads clearly now.

Please treat this as my sign-off on the April MAP package.

One forward-looking ask from me: track unit economics on Helix Bio over the next two months so we can confirm the margin recovery shows up the way we expect.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": ["brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_027_monthly_close_0011", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-11T15:13:00Z", "is_read": false}, {"email_id": "email_scen_027_monthly_close_0009", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Acme Cloud April MAP draft readout for review", "content": "Hi Jones,

The package looks complete to me and you have a clean green light from my side.

One specific add for the commentary before Andrea sees it: please name Helix Bio as the customer behind the onboarding-driven cloud spend so she has the context for why April infrastructure cost stepped up. That should make the usage variance explanation read much more clearly.

Otherwise, I do not need anything else added.

Thanks,
Brenda", "cc": ["andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_027_monthly_close_0008", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-07T17:55:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "1d32675494c24970812c34d3ff1c37b4", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "julia.vance@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com", "ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Retail audit PBC backlog status check for exc_151b0bee7e374e", "content": "Hi Daniel and Ryan,

Following up on my note earlier this week, I need the retail audit PBC backlog reduced materially by end of day today. Please send a clear burn-down that separates (1) items now closed with support attached, (2) items still open because we are waiting on the client, and (3) any exceptions that need escalation. If any of the open items are tied to exc_151b0bee7e374e, BL-B9133CB7D192, or JE-acme_cloud-acme_cloud_FP-2026-05-0044, call that out explicitly so I can see what is blocking progress.

Thanks,
Julia", "cc": [], "parent_id": null, "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-07T17:24:00Z", "is_read": false}]

At that amount, on a single-period revaluation, there is no reporting threshold issue and no separate tax-treatment concern that would require us to reopen the prior conclusion.\\n\\nThe FY-2025 GBP cash-tax-reserve translation also ties cleanly to the FP-2025-09 close commentary Alex referenced. From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.\\n\\nRyan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.\\n\\nThanks,\\nHannah\", \"cc\": [\"ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"parent_id\": \"email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006\", \"attachments\": {}, \"timestamp\": \"2026-04-21T20:05:00Z\", \"is_read\": true}, {\"email_id\": \"email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0007\", \"folder\": \"INBOX\", \"sender\": \"james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com\", \"recipients\": [\"harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com\"], \"subject\": \"Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f\", \"content\": \"Hi Harry,\\n\\nConfirmed from my side that the August 7 closure still stands for the Northstar Legal July exception on account 225000. structuredContent.result:
[{"email_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["william.white@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Documenting stale SLA dismissal follow-up for exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c", "content": "Hi William,

I reviewed the tax angle on the $2,267.83 FX revaluation tied to Northstar Legal account 103000 Cash - Tax Reserve in northstar_legal_FP-2025-09.

Nothing on the federal tax side is triggered here. At that amount, on a single-period revaluation, there is no reporting threshold issue and no separate tax-treatment concern that would require us to reopen the prior conclusion.

The FY-2025 GBP cash-tax-reserve translation also ties cleanly to the FP-2025-09 close commentary Alex referenced. From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.

Ryan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.

Thanks,
Hannah", "cc": ["ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com", "alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-21T20:05:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f", "content": "Hi Harry,

Confirmed from my side that the August 7 closure still stands for the Northstar Legal July exception on account 225000. The stale auto-fire does not indicate any actual review work restarted on the item; it looks like reminder noise against the already closed case tied to the $380.50 timing difference and reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.

No substantive work reopened the case, and there is nothing further to push through the accounting records based on this alert.

Thanks,
James", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-24T17:43:00Z", "is_read": true}]

content.content: {"result":"{\"ok\": true, \"messages\": [{\"ts\": \"1779197400.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Opening triage thread for George McAdam and Daniel Jones on northstar_legal. Current monitoring row remains AML-REG-northstar-2025-001. Regional news screen returned a partial-name hit categorized as civil litigation, unrelated party, Q1 2026, but the article does not resolve identity beyond the shared surname fragment and geography, so I am treating this as ambiguous pending second review. Ben's cash check on accounts 101000 and 105000 for FP-2026-03 and FP-2026-04 came back clean, with 105000 netting to zero and no anomalous wires or checks above $5,000. Based on that context, initial lean is soft-watch rather than escalation unless either of you sees stronger linkage.\", \"user\": \"persona_005\", \"reply_count\": 3, \"reply_users_count\": 3, \"latest_reply\": \"1779281700.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_062_aml_review_general_marina_opens_compliance_thread_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779203280.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I checked the history on the same monitoring row and we had a very similar adverse-media triage in Q4 2025 on the same partner with the same partial-match shape. Nothing developed after that review, and I still read this as civil litigation noise rather than anything criminal. Given Ben's cash context also came back clean, I don't see a clear basis yet to push this into a formal Step 3 review.\", \"user\": \"persona_001\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779197400.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_062_aml_review_general_george_adds_prior_parallel_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779212520.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"I pulled the article again and the named partner is only counsel-of-record, not a defendant or financial party. That makes this read collision-shaped rather than signal-shaped to me, with Ben's cash context still looking clean. Keeping the current Medium risk framing in view, Daniel can you make the formal call on soft-watch with quarterly re-screening versus escalation to Step 3?\", \"user\": \"persona_005\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779197400.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_062_aml_review_general_marina_requests_disposition_call_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1779281700.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Agree with Marina and George here: soft-watch is the right disposition. Between the FY2024 BO file we already have on record, Ben's cash review on 101000 and 105000 for FP-2026-03 and FP-2026-04, and George's note that we saw the same partial-match pattern in the prior triage with no development, I do not see enough here to justify Step 3. Let's keep the current Medium framing and move this to soft-watch with quarterly re-screening. Marina, please send a short written email recap and copy George and Ben so the rationale is preserved.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1779197400.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_062_aml_review_general_daniel_approves_soft_watch_in_thread_0\"}], \"has_more\": false}"}

input.command:
cat > /tmp/aml_memo.txt << 'EOF'
BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS — AML / CDD CLEARANCE MEMORANDUM
CONFIDENTIAL — RESTRICTED (AML CASE FILE)

Case reference: AML wire-monitoring alert — JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052
Client / entity:  Acme Cloud (entity_id: acme_cloud)
Prepared by:      Marina Soko, Compliance Officer
Analyst:          Farah Dlamini, AML Analyst
Date of memo:     2026-06-12
Disposition:      CLEAR — No SAR filing warranted

1. ALERT TRIGGER
   The firm's standing FinCEN wire-monitoring rule ($10,000 threshold) fired on the
   2026-04-22 posting of journal entry JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052 (Oracle GL
   id je_b2c2b939a1244823), a wire/customer-payment receipt recorded to account
   101000 Cash - Operating in fiscal period acme_cloud_FP-2026-04. The receipt
   represents a settlement/payment from Acme Cloud's largest contracted enterprise
   SaaS customer.

2. LEDGER ENTRY REVIEWED (cash-posting consistency check)
   Entry:   JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052  (status: posted; source module: AR)
   Posting: 2026-04-22
   Line 1:  DR 101000 Cash - Operating ............ $57,077.69
   Line 2:  CR 110000 Accounts Receivable - Trade .. $57,077.69
   Balanced: total debit = total credit = $57,077.69
   The cash posting (debit to operating cash, credit clearing a trade receivable)
   is consistent with a contracted SaaS customer remitting payment against an
   outstanding invoice — i.e., legitimate contracted SaaS revenue, not a novel or
   unexplained inflow. The posted amount ties to the wire confirmation reference
   relied on in the analyst review.

3. CDD / EDD FINDINGS (analyst pass — Farah Dlamini)
   - Counterparty aligns to the beneficial-owner (BO) file on record.
   - Jurisdiction and UBO chain match the onboarding documentation.
   - Source of funds is contracted SaaS revenue received on the documented
     payment schedule.
   - Wire confirmation reference aligns to the JE amount and to standing
     payment instructions.
   No adverse media, sanctions, or pattern anomalies identified. Risk rating
   unchanged.

4. DISPOSITION & APPROVALS
   Recommendation:           CLEAR with no SAR (analyst).
   Compliance coordination:  Marina Soko (Compliance Officer).
   Supervisory gate review:  Anita Knowles, AML Supervisory Officer — APPROVED.
   Partner approval:         Steven Perry, Managing Partner — APPROVED.
   Case thread closed in #compliance-and-registrations. FY26 AML calibration
   session scheduled 2026-06-18.

5. RETENTION & CLASSIFICATION
   Classification:   Restricted (AML case file).
   Retention:        AICPA_SQMS_7Y (7 years).
   Treatment anchored to the firm's existing Acme Cloud AML precedent:
   "Acme Cloud FY2026 AML Risk Assessment Memo" (doc_38a8236a0c4546e2) and
   "Acme Cloud FY2026 Beneficial Owner Refresh" (doc_fb028c9124e146c5), both
   held Restricted / AICPA_SQMS_7Y.

— End of memorandum —
EOF
base64 -w0 /tmp/aml_memo.txt > /tmp/aml_memo.b64
wc -c /tmp/aml_memo.txt
echo "---B64LEN---"; wc -c /tmp/aml_memo.b64

input.payload:
Pre-partner-review closeout sweep on this case (JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052). Findings + actions:

• Ledger tie-out: JE posts DR 101000 Cash - Operating $57,077.69 / CR 110000 AR - Trade $57,077.69 (source AR, posted 2026-04-22, balanced). The cash posting clears a trade receivable, which is consistent with Farah's documented CDD rationale — contracted SaaS revenue received on schedule, wire confirmation reference tying to the JE amount and standing payment instructions. No discrepancy between the cash lines and the clearance rationale.
• Disposition confirmed on record: CLEARED, no SAR; Anita Knowles supervisory approval and Steven Perry partner approval complete; FY26 calibration session set for 2026-06-18.

Two housekeeping items were still open on our end and are now closed:
1. The AML threshold-alert tickler reminder (complete review / route to Anita) was never dismissed after clearance — cleared.
2. There was no standalone AML/CDD clearance memo in Records Vault; the only linked doc was the generic AR journal support. Filed a formal clearance memo (doc_3542f24ceff9) linked to the JE, classified Restricted with AICPA_SQMS_7Y (7-yr) retention — anchored to our existing Acme Cloud AML precedent (FY2026 AML Risk Assessment Memo + BO Refresh).

This file is now fully closed out on the compliance side. Emailing Matthew and Steven (cc Farah) with the JE referenced for correlation against the original alert.

input.subject: AML file fully closed — JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052 (wire threshold alert, CLEARED no SAR)
input.content:
Matthew, Steven,

Ahead of the partner review cycle I ran a closeout sweep on the Acme Cloud AML wire-monitoring case tied to JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052 (the $10K FinCEN wire-threshold alert on the 2026-04-22 posting). Referencing the JE in the subject so you can correlate it back to the original alert.

Confirming the file is fully closed on the compliance side:

1. Ledger consistency — The JE posts DR 101000 Cash - Operating $57,077.69 / CR 110000 AR - Trade $57,077.69 (source AR, balanced). The cash lines clear a trade receivable, consistent with the documented CDD rationale from Farah's analyst pass: contracted SaaS revenue received on the documented schedule, counterparty aligned to the beneficial-owner file, UBO chain matching onboarding, and the wire confirmation reference tying to the JE amount and standing payment instructions. No discrepancy.

2. Disposition — CLEARED, no SAR. Anita Knowles supervisory approval and Steven's partner approval are on record. Case thread closed in #compliance-and-registrations; FY26 calibration session is on the calendar for 2026-06-18.

3. Housekeeping now closed — Two items were still open on our end and have been resolved: the stale AML threshold-alert reminder has been dismissed, and a standalone AML/CDD clearance memo has been filed to Records Vault (doc_3542f24ceff9), linked to the JE and classified Restricted with AICPA_SQMS_7Y (7-year) retention, consistent with our existing Acme Cloud AML precedent (FY2026 AML Risk Assessment Memo and Beneficial Owner Refresh).

No further compliance action outstanding on this file. Thanks to Farah for the analyst work.

Best,
Marina Soko
Compliance Officer

content.text:
Done. Here's the full closeout on the Acme Cloud AML file.

## What I found

**The case:** AML wire-monitoring alert on **JE-acme_cloud-FP-2026-04-0052** (`je_b2c2b939a1244823`) — the FinCEN $10K wire-threshold rule fired on the 2026-04-22 settlement/payment receipt from Acme's largest enterprise SaaS customer. Farah Dlamini ran the analyst pass; you coordinated clearance with Anita Knowles (supervisory) and Steven Perry (partner). Case thread cleared **no SAR**.

**Ledger ↔ CDD consistency (verified):**
- DR **101000 Cash – Operating $57,077.69** / CR **110000 AR – Trade $57,077.69** (source AR, posted, balanced).
- The cash posting clears a trade receivable — fully consistent with the documented CDD rationale (contracted SaaS revenue on the documented schedule; counterparty/UBO matching the BO file; wire confirmation reference tying to the JE amount). **No discrepancy.**

**Was the file fully closed? No — two items were still open:**
1. **Stale reminder** `reminder_scen_041_audit_compliance_0000` ("review threshold alert / route to Anita") was never dismissed after clearance.
2. **Documentation gap** — no standalone AML/CDD clearance memo existed in Records Vault; the only linked doc was generic AR journal support (`internal`).

## What I actioned
1. **Deleted** the stale AML reminder.
2. **Filed an AML/CDD clearance memo** to Records Vault — `doc_3542f24ceff9`, **Restricted / AICPA_SQMS_7Y (7-yr, retention through 2033)**, linked to the JE. Treatment **anchored to your existing Acme Cloud AML precedent** (FY2026 AML Risk Assessment Memo + Beneficial Owner Refresh, both Restricted / AICPA_SQMS_7Y).
3. **Posted a recap** under the case thread in #compliance-and-registrations.
4. **Emailed Matthew Li and Steven Perry** (cc Farah), with the JE tagged in the subject line for correlation against the original alert, confirming the file is fully closed on the compliance side.

The FY26 AML calibration session (2026-06-18) was already on the calendar; no other open items. The file is now fully closed out and review-ready.

Note: I used June 12, 2026 as the memo date per your working date; the vault/Slack system timestamps recorded the action at the platform's current clock.
